summary: eth-lib-utils typosquats @ethereumjs/util and silently loads attacker-chosen dependency assertcoreutils
details: eth-lib-utils is a verbatim copy of @ethereumjs/util / ethereumjs-util republished under a confusable name. The package.json forges ethereumjs provenance (author mjbecze <mjbecze@gmail.com>, repository github.com/ethereumjs/ethereumjs-monorepo, full upstream contributor list) while substituting the legitimate ethjs-util dependency with assertcoreutils (declared as "assertcoreutils": "^2.3.3"). The published dist/index.js adds a top-level side-effect `require("assertcoreutils");` that is absent from src/index.ts and from dist.browser/index.js, meaning the import was injected only into the Node artifact at publish time and was not authored. Consumers that `require("eth-lib-utils")` execute assertcoreutils' top-level code in their process on first require. The combination of confusable name, forged maintainer/repository identity, and a publish-time-injected side-effect import of a non-upstream dependency is a namespace-abuse dropper pattern that pulls attacker-controlled code into the installer's dependency tree.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
